info.description: OrderbookTrade High-performance matching engine API for order placement and market data.
info.title: OrderbookTrade Matching Engine API

EIP712Auth.name: Authorization
EIP712Auth.description: EIP-712 signature authentication via request body fields (maker_address, signature)

get.description: Check if the server is running
Generate a random nonce for EIP-712 signing

post.description: Login with EIP-712 signed message to receive a JWT token

get.description: WebSocket connection for real-time orderbook and trade updates. Subscribe to channels: orderbook, trades
Retrieve all markets with optional status filter

post.description: Create a new market (requires EIP-712 authentication)

get.description: Retrieve a specific market by its ID

put.description: Update an existing market (requires EIP-712 authentication)

get.description: Retrieve the current state of the order book (bids and asks) for a market
Retrieve a list of recent trades for a market
Retrieve the on-chain status of a market including condition and token IDs
Retrieve OHLCV candle data for charting

post.description: Place a new order with EIP-712 signature authentication

delete.description: Cancel an existing order by its ID with EIP-712 signature authentication

post.description: Admin endpoint to deposit funds into a user's account
Resolve a market with a winning outcome, trigger payouts

get.description: Get the resolution details and payouts for a resolved market
Get deposit history for the authenticated user
Get all token balances for the authenticated user
Get withdrawal history for the authenticated user

post.description: Deposit funds into the authenticated user's account
Request a withdrawal from the authenticated user's account

get.description: Get orders for the authenticated user with optional filters
Get positions for the authenticated user
Get trade history for the authenticated user

post.description: Start the market-making bot for a specific market

get.description: Get the current status of the market-making bot

timestamp.description: Unix timestamp (must be within 5 minutes of server time)
